description_long: This is a rectangular, soft-packaged snack product featuring a whimsical design with cartoon animal faces and characters printed across its white exterior. The front side displays a large, round, tan-colored animal mascot with a smiling face, surrounded by Japanese text and additional small illustrations. The sides of the package show more cartoon graphics and product information, including a yellow panel with text and a peach illustration. The back is covered in a repeating pattern of the same animal mascot and other small decorative elements. The packaging appears to be made of a flexible, matte plastic material, likely containing individually wrapped snacks inside. The overall appearance is playful and inviting, targeting a youthful or family-oriented audience.
description: A soft, rectangular snack package with playful cartoon animal designs and Japanese text on a white background, featuring a large mascot illustration on the front.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main mascot illustration, Japanese text, and several small cartoon graphics, making it the most visually detailed side.
description_view_1: This side view displays a single large cartoon animal graphic and product information in Japanese, with less visual clutter than the front.
description_view_2: The back view is covered in a repeating pattern of small cartoon animal faces and decorative elements, with no large central illustration.
description_view_3: The opposite side view features a yellow information panel, a peach illustration, and a cartoon animal, providing product details.
attribute_flavor_indication: A peach illustration is visible on one side, suggesting a peach flavor.
attribute_nutritional_labeling: A yellow information panel with Japanese text is present on one side, likely containing nutritional or ingredient information.
attribute_promotional_graphics: The package features a large cartoon animal mascot and several smaller character illustrations, which serve as promotional graphics to attract attention.
